prompt: A relay upgrade is producing two apparently unrelated symptoms. Events carrying false or zero-valued delivery metadata arrive without those fields, while events with non-ASCII labels intermittently fail checksum validation. The latter correlates with packets split by a proxy, but captures show the bytes are unchanged. ASCII events with truthy metadata pass when delivered as one buffer, and two complete ASCII frames in one buffer are a healthy control. Keep the four-byte length prefix, UTF-8 JSON payload, CRC trailer, and incremental feed API; callers may provide any byte-sized chunking and multiple frames per chunk. Do not weaken corruption detection or change the event schema.
